findings.evidence: The header says these declarations come from mathlib4#38292/#31449 at lines 17-23; grep confirms public same-name declarations at lines 44, 58, 81, 147, 267, 275, 281, 305, and 320 (`exists_monotone_partition_Icc`, `Path.codRestrict`, `Path.subpathOn`, quotient `subpathOn_*`, `Path.Homotopic.of_trans_symm`).
findings.fix: Keep vendored shims out of upstream namespaces or gate/remove them in the same change that updates imports, with TauCeti code switched to the non-conflicting replacement before the bump lands.
findings.issue: The prelude publicly defines pending upstream names in root/Mathlib namespaces, so a Mathlib bump that adds any of them will hit duplicate declarations before consumers can use the upstream API.

summary: The PR adds public API without removing existing TauCeti declarations, but the vendored prelude exposes pending Mathlib declarations under their eventual Mathlib/root names, making the next Mathlib bump predictably fail unless the shim is manually removed first.
